title: Hydrophace trisulca

taxonomy: Hydrophace trisulca is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Alismataceae, commonly known as the water-plantain family. It is native to North America and is often found in wetland areas, including marshes, swamps, and streams. The plant is typically identified by its distinctive three-part leaves and white or purple flowers. Hydrophace trisulca is also known as three-flowered water-plantain, which provides further insight into its classification. As a member of the Alismataceae family, it is closely related to other aquatic plants such as the bur-reed and the water-plantain.
